mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that is designed for ease of deployment and management using Juju. It is ideal for users looking for a robust Kubernetes solution that integrates well with Ubuntu and offers modern metrics and monitoring capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju for deployment and management, which may add complexity for users unfamiliar with it.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This step installs Juju on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Kubernetes Controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the Juju controller and deploys the Kubernetes control plane.

```bash

juju bootstrap <cloud> --config kubernetes-version=1.31

```

> Replace <cloud> with your cloud provider (e.g., aws, gcp, azure).

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes Bundle
steps.description:
This step deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle which includes all necessary components.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> Deploys the latest stable version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
Check the status of the deployed units to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> This command shows the status of all deployed units.

steps.title: Post-Installation Configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features such as monitoring and autoscaling as needed.

```bash

juju add-relation kubernetes-master:metrics prometheus:metrics

juju config kubernetes-worker enable-autoscaling=true

```

> Sets up monitoring with Prometheus.
> Enables autoscaling for worker nodes.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

juju destroy-controller <controller-name> --destroy-all-models

```

> Replace <controller-name> with your Juju controller name.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources that may remain after the controller is destroyed.

```bash

juju remove-application <app-name>

```

> Replace <app-name> with any remaining applications.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process will upgrade your Charmed Kubernetes deployment to the latest version.

```bash

juju backup

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes --revision <new-revision>

juju status

```

> Creates a backup of the current state.
> Replace <new-revision> with the desired version.
> Verify that the upgrade was successful.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified cloud provider is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud credentials and ensure they are set up correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unit is in error state'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if a charm fails to deploy due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the failing unit with 'juju debug-log' and ensure sufficient resources are available.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubernetes version not supported'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Kubernetes version is not compatible with the current Juju bundle.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are using a supported Kubernetes version as per the Charmed Kubernetes documentation.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.28

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'relation not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a required relation between charms is missing.

**Fix:**
Add the necessary relation using 'juju add-relation <charm1>:<relation> <charm2>:<relation>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features and improved charm relations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.29.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to check compatibility of custom charms.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'juju status' indicating all units are active and idle.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Juju installed and configured with access to your cloud provider.
